mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal disposable income directly increases consumer spending capacity, which flows into corporate earnings and drives positive equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) when RSAFS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, borrowing costs for corporations and households rise sharply, compressing profit margins and triggering immediate equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer confidence, generating positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or forward returns are negative in over 60% of co-occurring instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in the index of industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing output and reduced capacity utilization, which depresses corporate revenues and yields negative equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods following an INDPRO decline, or the mechanism shows zero predictive power during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: When new housing starts fall below 1.8 million units annually, it signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and related consumer spending, triggering immediate negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in over 65% of observations where HOUST drops below 1800, invalidating the contraction signal.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates combined with rising unemployment create stagflationary pressure that forces central banks to pause easing, resulting in negative equity returns over the next month as growth fears dominate.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative Rank IC, or forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth alongside stable inflation indicates robust supply-side expansion that supports corporate margins, driving positive equity returns over a quarter during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in over 60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance when CPIAUCSL exceeds 205.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth directly correlates with rising corporate earnings expectations, producing a consistent positive drift in equity markets over the subsequent month regardless of short-term noise.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates.
